page: Microsoft Azure D4s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor is equipped with a 35 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark evaluations indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside notable multi-core and memory bandwidth constraints. Single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench falls within the average range, as does single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower-tier multi-core Geekbench and stress-ng scores. Memory subsystem performance shows a significant bottleneck, with uncached large-block read bandwidth dropping to a poor 17.74 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 15.28 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET throughput also align with the lower performance quartiles.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4s_v4 offers a standard resource density profile for general-purpose workloads that do not demand high memory throughput or sustained parallel processing. The absence of local storage and complimentary public IPv4 addresses positions it as a basic utility node. It is appropriate for lightweight web serving, basic text processing, single-threaded utility scripts, and small-scale development or testing environments where multi-core scaling and high-speed local I/O are not critical requirements.
description: The Microsoft Azure D4s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv4 family, powered by a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance features a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show average single-core execution and text processing capabilities, contrasted by weak multi-core scaling and poor uncached memory bandwidth. The instance is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads with moderate resource demands, such as basic web serving, development environments, and lightweight utility tasks, where high-throughput parallel processing and local disk I/O are not required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering balanced resources for standard workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, suitable for basic utility and development workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and standard memory allocation for utility workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Average single-core performance for text processing and basic compression
No local storage or integrated GPUs, relying on remote Premium Storage
Weak multi-core scaling and limited uncached memory bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight web serving, development environments, and utility tasks
